reasoning: With GPT-5 recently released in August 2025 and explicitly confirmed by CEO Sam Altman to be 'missing many important attributes' of AGI, the timeline is extremely tight for any new model to launch and be declared AGI by year's end (a roughly 2-month window). Furthermore, officially announcing AGI would trigger the Microsoft AGI clause—severely restricting Microsoft's access to OpenAI's IP—creating a massive financial and structural disincentive to make such a declaration prematurely, especially amidst ongoing restructuring into a Public Benefit Corporation. Altman's rhetoric has pivoted to calling AGI a 'sloppy term' where the exact point of arrival 'will matter much less', leaning away from a dramatic, binary announcement. Given the market priced this at 4% in mid-October, the probability of an official announcement in 2025 is very low.
